title: The Bottled Water Paradox: Why Sales Keep Rising
subtitle: 瓶装水销量为何逆势增长

paragraphs.en: For all the innovation and choice that define the food and drink industries, if you want to make money, you could do a lot worse than fill a bottle with water and sell it. A litre of tap water, the stuff we have cleverly piped into our homes, costs less than half a penny. A litre of bottled water can cost well over a pound, especially for something fancy that has been sucked through a mountain.
paragraphs.zh: 尽管食品饮料行业以创新和选择多样著称，但若想赚钱，把水装瓶出售绝对是个好主意。一升自来水——我们巧妙引入家中的水——成本不到半便士。而一升瓶装水售价可能远超一英镑，尤其是那些号称从山间汲取的高端水。

paragraphs.en: Yet the bottled water market is livelier than ever. It defies our increasing awareness of the harm plastics do to the environment and a broader, growing sense that something has to change. Sales in the UK were worth a record £558.4 million this past year, an increase of 7%, according to the latest figures from the market analyst Kantar. Separate data from other analysts show that last year the British consumed more than 2.2 billion litres of bottled water, including take-home and on-the-go products. That's an annual rise in volume of 8.5%.
paragraphs.zh: 然而瓶装水市场比以往更加活跃。这有悖于我们对塑料危害环境日益增长的认识，也与“必须有所改变”的普遍呼声背道而驰。据市场分析公司Kantar最新数据，过去一年英国销售额创下5.584亿英镑的纪录，增长7%。其他分析师的单独数据则显示，去年英国人消费了超过22亿升瓶装水，包括家庭装和便携装产品，年销量增幅达8.5%。

paragraphs.en: Environmental campaigners are struggling to understand why nations blessed with clean tap water grow only fonder of the bottle. 'It's very surprising to me,' says Sam Chetan-Walsh, a political adviser at Greenpeace and campaigner against ocean plastic. 'Public awareness has never been higher, but the message is not quite reaching all the people it needs to.' Where it is heard, the message is causing concern. Plastic water bottles require oceans of fossil fuels to make and ship. Additionally, single-use plastics of all types are polluting our cities and seas. Numerous documentaries have shown how plastic is ultimately killing wildlife.
paragraphs.zh: 环保人士难以理解，为何拥有清洁自来水的国家却愈发钟爱瓶装水。绿色和平组织政治顾问兼反海洋塑料活动家萨姆·切坦-沃尔什表示：“这让我非常惊讶。公众意识空前高涨，但信息并未完全触达所有需要的人。”在信息被接收的地方，它引发了忧虑。塑料水瓶的生产和运输消耗大量化石燃料。此外，各类一次性塑料正在污染我们的城市和海洋。无数纪录片已展示塑料最终如何杀死野生动物。

paragraphs.en: Moves against various plastics have gathered pace, from shopping bags to straws and plastic-lined coffee cups. Chetan-Walsh argues that bottled water is different because the alternatives are so obvious. 'If a product that is so nakedly unnecessary can exist, then the whole system is failing,' he says. Hope is not entirely out of reach. The rate of growth has begun to ease (sales were up 7% in the year, compared with 8% the previous year).
paragraphs.zh: 从购物袋到吸管再到塑料内衬咖啡杯，反对各类塑料的行动正在加速。切坦-沃尔什认为瓶装水与众不同，因为替代品如此显而易见。他说：“如果一种完全不必要的产品能存在，那么整个体系就是失败的。”希望并非完全遥不可及。增长速度已开始放缓（当年销售额增长7%，上年为8%）。

paragraphs.en: But even if large numbers of people are quitting bottled water because of care for the environment, others are taking it up. The introduction of the sugar tax on juices and soda drinks has pushed more people to bottled water, while health awareness has boosted its desirability. Tap water consumption is growing at roughly the same pace (UK consumers still drink almost three times as much tap water as bottled water).
paragraphs.zh: 但即便大量人群因环保而放弃瓶装水，仍有人开始消费它。对果汁和汽水征收的“糖税”使更多人转向瓶装水，同时健康意识也提升了其吸引力。自来水消费量正以大致相同的速度增长（英国消费者饮用的自来水仍是瓶装水的近三倍）。

paragraphs.en: So the plastic tide only creeps higher. The industry is quick to point out that all its bottles are recyclable. 'But collection rates are, at the most generous estimates, 56%, so the actual recycling rate will be lower than that,' Chetan-Walsh says. And while bottles may be recyclable, very few are made of recycled plastic. One water bottle company launched recycled half-litre eco bottles alongside its standard bottles. Another has pledged to use only recycled plastic across its range by 2025. Chetan-Walsh believes in a ban on single-use bottles. Bans do exist in some places. Organizers of a famous music festival announced that water bottles will not be sold this summer. San Francisco has banned them from city property and events. Last year, the UK government set out plans to ban single-use plastic from its estate.
paragraphs.zh: 于是塑料浪潮只涨不落。行业迅速指出所有瓶子都是可回收的。切坦-沃尔什说：“但即使按最乐观的估计，回收率也仅为56%，所以实际回收率会更低。”而且尽管瓶子可回收，但很少有瓶子由再生塑料制成。一家水瓶公司在标准瓶之外推出了半升再生“生态瓶”。另一家公司承诺到2025年全系列产品仅使用再生塑料。切坦-沃尔什主张禁用一次性瓶子。某些地方确有禁令。一个著名音乐节的主办方宣布今夏不再出售瓶装水。旧金山已禁止在城市物业和活动中使用。去年，英国政府制定了在其管辖范围内禁用一次性塑料的计划。

paragraphs.en: Water bottlers, unsurprisingly, don't support bans. But they raise concerns about health rather than profit margins. Last month one chief executive of a water bottling company said that bans would result in greater consumption of sugary drinks, adding to all the health dangers of obesity, diabetes and tooth decay. Kinvara Carey, general manager of an association of the biggest bottled water manufacturers, cites a survey in which people were asked what they would do if bottled water were not available. 'Forty-four per cent would buy another drink, which is not great, 14% would go without and 4.5% said they would find a fountain,' she says. 'The choice is important.'
paragraphs.zh: 不出所料，瓶装水企业不支持禁令。但他们提出的担忧关乎健康而非利润。上月，一家瓶装水公司首席执行官称，禁令将“导致含糖饮料消费增加，加重肥胖、糖尿病和蛀牙等健康风险”。最大瓶装水制造商协会的总经理金瓦拉·凯里引用一项调查，询问人们若没有瓶装水会怎么做。她说：“44%的人会买其他饮料，这不理想；14%的人会忍着不喝；4.5%的人说会找饮水机。选择权很重要。”

paragraphs.en: What if fountains were more numerous, and tap water more clearly available in cafes, restaurants and elsewhere? Dozens of fountains are being installed in London. There are similar initiatives elsewhere. Before plastic and the marketing that made people think they needed bottled water in the first place, fountains were an urban fixture. Greenpeace, among others, is also pushing for a deposit return scheme. This would mean tax on bottled water would be refunded to customers who returned the plastic for recycling.
paragraphs.zh: 如果公共饮水机更多、咖啡馆和餐厅等场所的自来水供应更便利，会怎样？伦敦正在安装数十台饮水机。其他地方也有类似举措。在塑料以及那套让人们误以为需要瓶装水的营销出现之前，饮水机本是城市的标配。绿色和平组织等也在推动“押金返还”计划，即顾客归还塑料瓶回收后可获得瓶装水税费返还。

paragraphs.en: Even if bottled water sales are growing slightly more slowly, the industry is racing to adapt to changing concerns and tastes. Flavoured water is booming: sales of the sparkling variety shot up by 20%, according to the latest analyst data. Meanwhile, international water brands, as well as a range of new companies, are selling high-end reusable bottles. And if you must fill them with tap water, why not add flavouring?
paragraphs.zh: 即便瓶装水销量增速略微放缓，行业仍在竞相适应不断变化的关切与口味。风味水正在蓬勃发展：最新分析师数据显示，气泡风味水销量飙升了20%。与此同时，国际水品牌及一系列新公司正在销售高端可重复使用的瓶子。如果你非得用自来水装满它们，何不加点调味剂呢？

paragraphs.en: As the owner of multiple sugary drink brands and bottled water, PepsiCo is facing challenges on health and environmental fronts. Last year, the company bought SodaStream (a drinks company that sells machines for making tap water bubbly and then consumers add flavours) for $3.2 billion. It also launched a range of fancy bottles that work with tap water and flavour packets. The bottle is reusable. The packets? Not so much, and, yes, they are made of plastic, although the company invites users to post them back for recycling.
paragraphs.zh: 作为多个含糖饮料品牌和瓶装水的拥有者，百事可乐在健康和环境方面面临挑战。去年，公司以32亿美元收购了SodaStream（一家销售可将自来水制成气泡水再添加风味的机器的饮料公司）。它还推出了一系列配合自来水和风味包使用的高端水瓶。瓶子可重复使用，但风味包呢？没那么环保，而且它们确实由塑料制成，尽管公司鼓励用户寄回回收。

paragraphs.en: As is so often the case, clever marketing can beat reason; awareness is rarely enough. 'There is always this kind of slip between concern, intent and changed behaviour,' says Giles Quick, an analyst of bottled water. 'The best example is five a day, the recommendation to have at least five portions of fruit and vegetables every day. Almost everyone is aware of this, but something like 15% of us achieve it.' Unless a far-reaching bottle ban does come into force, it will be up to consumers to not only demand change — but to act themselves.
paragraphs.zh: 正如常见的情形，聪明的营销可以战胜理性；仅有意识远远不够。瓶装水分析师贾尔斯·奎克说：“在关注、意图和行为改变之间总是存在落差。最好的例子是‘每天五份’，即建议每日至少摄入五份蔬果。几乎人人知晓，但真正做到的只有约15%。”除非影响深远的瓶装禁令真正生效，否则消费者不仅要呼吁改变，更要亲自行动。
